docs.creator: Grand, P. M. (Paule Marie), 1914-
description: Examination of prehistoric art ; includes discussion of technique and dating ; brief section devoted to Aboriginal art in Australia (p. 71-73)

Paleolithic mural painting : discovery and interpretation -- The exploration of the caves -- Classic interpretations : art and hunting magic -- New theories : analysis of the signs -- Paleolithic mural painting : style, technique and dating -- Rock painting and carving : Mediterranean, Levantine, African and Australian -- The Mediterranean Province -- The Spanish Levant -- Southern Africa -- The Sahara -- Australia -- Engraving, sculpture and "art mobilier" -- Engraving -- Sculpture -- "Art Mobilier."

docs.title: The art of the Wandjina; aboriginal cave paintings in Kimberley, Western Australia

docs.creator: McGregor, William
description: Sketch of Gunin phonology, morphology and syntax spoken by Kwini people of Kalumburu, includes two short texts and notes on relationship of Gunin to other Kimberley languages; brief histories of the missions at Kalumburu and Forrest River and decimation of population by disease and massacres; cultural notes on social organisation, conception and mortuary beliefs and Wandjina mythology
"Gunin is not to be confused with Gooniyandi (also sometimes spelt Gunian), which language is spoken four hundred kilometres ... to the south."--Page 5, footnote
docs.title: Gunin/Kwini

docs.creator: Arden, Harvey
description: A book that touches the soul, Harvey Arden's Dreamkeepers: A Spirit-Journey into Aboriginal Australia transports us through vivid narrative and haunting photographs into the world and minds of the custodians of the oldest culture on the planet. Coauthor of the best-selling Wisdomkeepers: Meetings with Native American Spiritual Elders, Arden journeys in Dreamkeepers to the Aboriginal peoples of Australia's wild and spectacular Kimberly region - the "Land Beyond Goodbye"--Intent on his continuing life-quest or spirit-journey for what he calls "the Aboriginal in each of us." Though a series of fascinating meetings, interspersed with hair-raising adventures and misadventures of outback travel, we fall under the spell of a remarkable gallery of Aboriginal men and women both ordinary and extraordinary - rough-edged stockmen and gold hunters, storytellers and healers, poets and artists, political visionaries and mystic philosophers, spiritual elders and traditional Law Men. Arden asks of them only to share with us whatever it is they care to share. And from each we receive the gift of some poignant memory, penetrating insight, or shard of universal wisdom. What emerges is an unparalleled close-up portrait of a proud and courageous people who, against all odds, have clung - if only, at times, by the fingernails - to their ancient metaphysical concept of the Dreamtime, or Dreaming that "time outside of time, a dimension of Being somehow interpenetrating and concurrent with our own," in which the Ancestor Beings continuously "sing the world into existence." For those who enjoyed Bruce Chatwin's The Songlines, this remarkable real-life journey to the "edges of the dreamtime"--combining a vivid adventure narrative with a moving personal spiritual odyssey - will provide a rich new trove of unforgettable experience, meaning, and inspiration
docs.title: Dreamkeepers : a spirit-journey into aboriginal Australia

docs.creator: Finley, Carol
description: Introduction to Aboriginal art: Dreaming -- Bark paintings of Arnhem Land: Mimi myth -- Stars -- Animals in painting -- Tracks and footprints -- Kimberley region: Wandjina -- Weather -- Christianity -- Desert paintings: Animal tracks -- Dreamtime -- Conclusion
Describes the art of the Australian Aborigines including rock painting and engraving as well as sand and bark painting; also discusses the symbolism found in these works
docs.title: Aboriginal art of Australia : exploring cultural traditions
